Server._comment: TCP port the bridge listens on. Override with `--port N` on the command line; CLI wins over this value.

Tracing._comment: Per-request audit JSON capture. OFF by default — turn on to debug a specific issue, then turn off (the JSON files contain full request and response bodies, including user prompts). Each request emits 4 files (inbound-req, inbound-resp, upstream-req, upstream-resp) sharing the same seq. Directory is relative to the .exe unless absolute.

Routing._comment: nginx-style locations. Each location is a self-contained closure: 'When' matches (leaves: Model/Effort/Header; composites: AllOf/AnyOf; top-level fields = implicit AllOf), 'Use' fully describes what to do (Model swap, EffortMap remapping, Headers Set/Remove). First-match-wins; no chain, no fall-through. Header Set/Remove restricted to a whitelist (anthropic-beta, Editor-Version, Editor-Plugin-Version, Copilot-Integration-Id, X-GitHub-Api-Version). Use 'anthropic-beta:pattern' in Remove for token-level strip with trailing '*' wildcard. NOTE: the opus-4.6/4.7 '+1M beta -> dedicated -1m variant' redirects were REMOVED in the 2026 model reconciliation. Copilot retired the claude-opus-4.6-1m and claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal ids (they 400 'not available for integrator'), and the opus-4.6/4.7 BASE ids now serve 1M context natively (probed: 600k+ token prompts return 200), so no redirect is needed -- the 1M beta passes through and the base model handles it, same as opus-4.8 and sonnet-5.
Routing._Locations_disabled_comment: DISABLED rule set (the config binder ignores any key not named exactly 'Locations', so a leading-underscore key is how this project 'comments out' JSON). To ENABLE: rename '_Locations_disabled' below to 'Locations' and rename the real 'Locations': [] above to something else (e.g. '_Locations_off'). Exactly one 'Locations' key may be active.

_Locations_disabled.Note: DISABLED: route Claude Code's claude-opus-4.8 traffic to Copilot gpt-5.6-sol. EffortMap max->xhigh is the operator's DELIBERATE down-tier here: unlike gpt-5.5, gpt-5.6-sol (the 'xlarge' effort profile) DOES accept 'max' natively, so without this map Claude Code's 'max' would pass through verbatim; the map caps it at xhigh instead. Remove the EffortMap to let 'max' reach gpt-5.6-sol unchanged. gpt-5.6-sol's Claude Code tool-protocol fit is verified end to end (tests/CopilotBridge.Playground/Headless/CcOnGpt56HeadlessTests.cs -- multi-tool task, all /responses 200, tools on the wire, canary round-trip); the runaway circuit-breaker (see docs/gpt55-runaway-diagnosis.md) still guards every response regardless. Still a cross-model substitution -- enable only when you intend Copilot's gpt-5.6-sol to serve Claude Code traffic.

CcToResponses._comment: Compatibility policy applied ONLY when a Claude Code /cc request is translated to a Copilot Responses backend (for example, claude-opus-4.8 routed to gpt-5.6-sol). Claude Code includes its Agent delegation tool in sub-agent requests; GPT treats it as an ordinary repeatable function and can recursively fan out hundreds of agents even though each response is individually valid. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
CcToResponses._PreventRecursiveAgentDelegation: When true (default), a request carrying x-claude-code-agent-id is classified as a sub-agent and its exact Agent tool is omitted from the translated upstream /responses tools array. Root Claude Code requests retain Agent. Native /cc Anthropic passthrough and native /codex requests are unchanged. Set false only if recursive GPT-backed sub-agent delegation is intentional and externally bounded.

OutboundBeta._comment: anthropic-beta tokens stripped from EVERY outbound request, regardless of model. Patterns use the same trailing-* wildcard as Headers.Remove. advisor-tool-* is stripped because Copilot hard-400s it ('unsupported beta header(s): advisor-tool-2026-03-01') on every model. NOTE: structured-outputs-* was previously stripped too (a GCP Vertex org policy 400'd it), but a 2026-07 live probe found Copilot now accepts structured-outputs-2025-12-15 (200 on opus-4.8), so it is forwarded by default. A Vertex-backed tenant that still gets FAILED_PRECONDITION can add 'structured-outputs-*' back here.

UpstreamRetry._comment: Idempotent retry of transient upstream connection failures (net_http_client_execution_error / net_http_ssl_connection_failed) on POST /v1/messages. Only retries when the failure occurs BEFORE response headers arrive (request body never processed upstream, so re-sending is safe) — never mid-stream. MaxRetries=0 disables. Backoff: BaseDelayMs * BackoffMultiplier^(attempt-1), clamped to MaxDelayMs.

UpstreamTimeout._comment: Two independent INACTIVITY (idle) budgets on BOTH upstream forward paths — /cc (Anthropic passthrough) and Codex (Responses) — NOT a total-duration cap. As long as Copilot keeps making progress the relevant timer resets, so a legitimately slow-but-progressing request is never aborted. Fills the gap left by HttpClient.Timeout, which (under ResponseHeadersRead) bounds only the wait for headers and never the SSE body reads. Only the mid-stream surfacing differs by path (see StreamIdleAction/StreamIdleSignal — /cc injects a retryable error; Codex flushes response.failed). Each budget <= 0 disables that phase (no timer armed, zero overhead). Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
UpstreamTimeout._FirstByteTimeoutSeconds: Idle budget for Copilot to return response headers (the first byte), measured PER send attempt (retry backoff does not count against it). A near-full-context prompt legitimately takes minutes to first byte, so this is generous. On timeout, since no bytes reached the client, the bridge returns a real 504 Gateway Timeout. Default 240 (4 min): under the 10-min HttpClient.Timeout, above a realistic cache-creation first byte. <= 0 disables (falls back to HttpClient.Timeout).
UpstreamTimeout._StreamIdleTimeoutSeconds: Idle budget for the gap between consecutive SSE events once the stream started; reset on every event, so a stream that keeps emitting is never aborted. Default 60 remains below Claude Code's own opt-in stream watchdog (CLAUDE_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS, default 90s). A timeout observation is right-censored at cancellation and cannot prove whether upstream would later resume, so tune this separately from failure recovery. <= 0 disables.
UpstreamTimeout._StreamIdleAction: What to do to the client stream when the stream-idle budget fires mid-response (headers already sent, wire status stays 200). Retry (default) injects a retryable error event so Claude Code discards the partial streaming attempt and issues its non-streaming fallback request; buffered T3 translates a cross-routed Responses body into Anthropic IR before response detectors run. Truncate ends the stream with no error event (silent cut-short 200) for operators who do not want recovery.
UpstreamTimeout._StreamIdleSignal: Retryable error wire shape when StreamIdleAction=Retry. OverloadedError (default) => overloaded_error (Claude Code retries; 3 consecutive => opus->Sonnet fallback). ApiError => api_error/500.

PoisonedContext._comment: Request-side observe-only scan for a poisoned transcript: detects a single tool stuck in a repeated-failure loop (its failed tool_results — is_error, or content starting with 'API Error:'/'Error:' — replayed by the client every turn). Structural, NOT phrase-matching: it keys on 'one tool name accumulating many failures', so it survives whatever error wording Copilot returns (region 400, quota, timeout, model-not-supported, ...). It never blocks or mutates the request — a heavily poisoned context can only be cured by the user compacting the session, and stripping the blocks would 400 upstream. The total failure count is recorded on the summary line (poisoned_tool_results=); when one tool's failures reach WarnThreshold the stage logs one WARNING naming that tool and telling the user to /compact. The bad output such a context can induce is caught separately by Detectors:RunawayGuard. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
PoisonedContext._WarnThreshold: Failed tool_results attributable to a SINGLE tool in one request, at/above which the 'compact your session' WARNING fires. Keyed on the worst single tool (not the grand total) so 'one tool looping' warns but 'a few unrelated one-off failures' does not. The gpt-5.5 runaway had one tool (Agent) fail 50x. <= 0 warns on any single failure.

Detectors._comment: Response-side detectors, run in one pass over the SSE stream. DoneFilter (drops the [DONE] terminator) is always on and has no config. The detector sections below are configurable.

ModelRewrite._comment: When true, rewrites `message.model` in the response (both buffered and the message_start SSE event) back to the model id the client originally requested — so Claude Code's session log and ccusage report claude-opus-4-8 instead of the back-end variant the bridge routed to. Turn off to let downstream tooling see the real back-end model.

ResponseLeakGuard._BufferScannableBlocks: Opt-in airtight streaming suppression. When true (and PreserveStream true) the guard withholds each scannable content block's deltas (every text and thinking block, unconditionally — withholding is NOT gated on ScanThinking; that switch only controls whether the withheld thinking block is scanned) until the block closes, scans it, and relays it only if clean — so a detected leak is aborted BEFORE any leaked byte reaches the client. Non-scannable blocks (tool_use/input_json_delta) still stream live. Default false = pre-existing relay-until-detection (a detected leak's bytes may already be on the client). No effect when PreserveStream is false or the guard is disabled. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing it.
ResponseLeakGuard._Signal: Error emitted to force a retry when a leaked tool call is detected. OverloadedError => overloaded_error (HTTP 529): retried, and Claude Code falls back to Sonnet after 3 in a row. ApiError => api_error (HTTP 500): retried without a model fallback.
ResponseLeakGuard._Signatures: Per-signature on/off switches. Turn one off to clear a false positive (e.g. you're discussing how <invoke> tool-use, a <task-notification> envelope, or the <system-reminder> wrapper works and the model echoes a sample) while leaving the rest of the guard active. The tripped signature and the exact key to disable it are named in both the retry error and the WARNING log. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing any switch.

ToolInputValidation._comment: Validates real tool_use input JSON against the declared tool schema after the tool block closes, and records tool_input_invalid=true on the request summary. OBSERVE-ONLY BY DEFAULT: it does NOT abort, because Claude Code recovers from an invalid tool call natively (it parses input with safeParseJSON - malformed JSON falls back to {} - then zod strictObject.safeParse, and on failure feeds the model an is_error tool_result so it retries with corrected input). Aborting was found to cut off that self-heal (e.g. a real AskUserQuestion emitted without the required 'question' field, which CC would have recovered from, instead surfaced 'Server error mid-response'). Set MalformedJsonAction / SchemaViolationAction to an Abort* value only for a backend/tool combo where CC does NOT self-heal. Read at startup - RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
ToolInputValidation._MalformedJsonAction: Action when the accumulated tool input is not valid JSON. Observe (default) = record and relay (CC's safeParseJSON returns null -> {} -> schema re-prompt). AbortOverloaded = abort with overloaded_error/529. AbortApiError = abort with api_error/500. The wire shape is chosen by the action; there is no separate Signal key.
ToolInputValidation._SchemaViolationAction: Action when the tool input is valid JSON but violates the declared schema (missing required field, wrong type). Observe (default) = record and relay (CC's strictObject.safeParse re-prompts the model). AbortOverloaded = abort with overloaded_error/529. AbortApiError = abort with api_error/500.
ToolInputValidation._PreserveStream: Only relevant when an action is an Abort* value. true (default) injects the error mid-stream; false buffers the whole response and returns a real HTTP status before any bytes reach the client.

RunawayGuard._comment: Volume/degeneracy circuit-breaker on the response (streaming AND buffered application/json): aborts a degenerate-generation runaway (a model stuck emitting an unbounded stream of tiny fragments, OR repeating one token) before it hangs the client for minutes. Trips on any of the four signals below (two volume budgets, a sliding-window repetition-density ratio, and a consecutive-run count). On a trip it emits the same retryable overloaded_error as ResponseLeakGuard and logs `runaway=true` on the request summary. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
RunawayGuard._MaxDeltaBytes: Cumulative content_block_delta payload bytes across the whole response. A real gpt-5.5 runaway hit ~17 MB; this is set above any legitimate single response so normal large outputs pass.
RunawayGuard._MaxDeltaCount: Per-content-block ceiling on content_block_delta events. The degenerate signature is one tool call emitting tens of thousands of tiny fragments (the runaway hit 27,643 in one block). <= 0 disables this check (byte budget still applies).
RunawayGuard._RepetitionWindow: Sliding-window size (whitespace tokens) for the per-content-block repetition signal. Trips when the trailing N tokens are FULL and their unique-token ratio is below RepetitionMinUniqueRatio. Catches a single-token loop (observed: claude-opus-4.8 repeating one token ~32,000x to max_tokens in ~1,010 deltas / ~500 KB) that stays under both volume budgets. <= 0 disables the repetition signal. Clamped to 100000 max (the ring is allocated per request, so an absurd value can't OOM the bridge).
RunawayGuard._RepetitionMinUniqueRatio: Unique-token-ratio floor: trip when distinct/RepetitionWindow < this. MUST be in the open interval (0, 1); a value <= 0 or >= 1 is out of range and SILENTLY DISABLES the repetition signal (it is not clamped) - so e.g. 5 (a typo for 0.5) is a no-op, not a stricter setting. 0.05 is ~25x above the observed runaway ratio (~0.002) and ~17x below a normal diverse response (~0.88).
RunawayGuard._RepetitionMaxConsecutiveRepeat: Per-content-block ceiling on CONSECUTIVE identical tokens. Trips when the same token repeats this many times in a row, independent of RepetitionWindow fullness and of total length — catches a SHORT flood (observed: claude-opus-4.8 repeating one token ~100x in a 108-token buffered body) that never fills the window and so is invisible to RepetitionMinUniqueRatio. Runs on BOTH streaming and buffered paths. Reset per block. 50 by default and deliberately low (50-in-a-row is already degenerate); <= 0 disables it. A legitimate repetitive output that trips it is fixed by RAISING this value, not by disabling the guard.
RunawayGuard._Signal: Error emitted on a trip. OverloadedError => overloaded_error (HTTP 529): retryable. ApiError => api_error (HTTP 500).
